title: Everyone’s Been Drawing Pterosaur Wings Wrong
gist: A Paleobiology study using 'theoretical morphospace' mapped the possible wing shapes of nine pterosaur species and found that existing reconstructions cluster too narrowly, likely failing to reflect the true diversity of pterosaur wing configurations.

insights.claim: University of Bristol paleontologists used 'theoretical morphospace' to map all possible wing types for nine pterosaur species and found existing reconstructions cluster, likely not reflecting the actual diversity of pterosaur wings.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Method critique of paleoart accuracy; basic paleontology with no market read.
insights.quote: they likely don’t reflect the diversity of pterosaur wings that actually existed
insights.locator: They then mapped the current reconstructions on top

insights.claim: Preserved pterosaur wing structures are rare and incomplete despite plentiful skeletons, forcing reconstructions to rely on best guesses.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Explains the inherent uncertainty in depicting extinct flyers.
insights.quote: preserved wing structures are relatively rare and tend to be incomplete
insights.locator: While there are plenty of pterosaur skeletons
